issues.detail: The round-2 laundering hole remains. Git ancestry proves topology, not timing: a pre-pull commit already on the recorded branch can descend from the recorded base and pass E1. Likewise, implementation already present in the recorded base can be laundered by a later unrelated non-empty PR whose tests pass. PR-only main blocks direct pushes but not either case, and “one PR ↔ one issue” has no issue-scope check.
issues.fix: Have /next create or verify a clean branch whose recorded tip equals the fetched base OID, and refuse pre-existing branch commits or task work. At /done, require a concise founder-confirmed mapping from the PR diff to the issue UUID/owned ATs; the qualifying work must be present in that post-pull diff.

issues.detail: E2 says the verifier is pinned by a manifest revision recorded in the pull record, but the defined pull-record fields omit that revision; it exists only in the local binding cache. After cache loss or remote adoption, /done and R3 cannot reconstruct which tests and runner were authoritative at pull, contradicting the remote-evidence-first claim.
issues.fix: Embed the immutable manifest revision and verify-set digest in the Linear pull comment. Run its exact verifier from a clean temporary checkout against the merge OID, and record that revision, command, environment/toolchain identity, results, and durable log in the completion bundle.

issues.detail: R1 scans origin/main merge commits, but activation does not require merge-commit-only PRs. A routine squash or rebase merge therefore defeats the promised forgotten-/done recovery even though /done itself could validate the GitHub PR.
issues.fix: Either configure merge-commit-only PR merges as an activation prerequisite or make R1 enumerate GitHub PRs merged since the cursor and verify each reported merge OID is reachable from origin/main, independent of merge strategy.
